heroHeadline: CJNG Internal War Enters Week 2 as Violence Cascades Across Colima, Jalisco, Zacatecas
heroSubtitle: Day 55 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco. The CJNG 'fractura total' — El Jardinero/El Sapo vs. O3/Los Cuinis — has produced a cascade of security incidents in its first week: an armed CJNG convoy attacked a Zacatecas police headquarters (Apr 12); new mass grave discoveries at Rancho Izaguirre (CJNG training camp, Teuchitlán) pushed the evidence timeline back 11 years (Apr 12–14); eight Manzanillo municipal police were arrested for leaking real-time C5i intelligence to CJNG via the Threema encrypted app (Apr 16); and three CJNG gunmen were killed in a joint security pursuit on the Colima-Manzanillo corridor (Apr 17). O3 retains Grupo Élite and Los Cuinis financial networks; El Jardinero holds multi-state regional plazas; El Sapo manages port logistics through Puerto Vallarta. No direct inter-faction combat in the CJNG heartland yet — both factions appear in active buildup phase. Operativo Enjambre cumulative total: 75+ officials detained. FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match: 57 days away (June 11, New York/New Jersey). Guadalajara's Akron Stadium hosts four group-stage matches.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, France 24, CGTN, Al Jazeera, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, El Financiero, El Imparcial, Borderland Beat, RED Michoacán, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 18, 2026.
